LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT YICK WONG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ISSUES BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S HAVE NOT CARRIED THEIR BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT NO ACTIONABLE CONDUCT OCCURRED WITHIN THE APPLICABLE STATUTORY PERIODS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THE PLEADING CANNOT SUPPORT THE CAUSE OF ACTION OR THAT A PRIVILEGE NECESSARILY BARS THE CLAIM. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S HAVE NOT CARRIED THEIR BURDEN AND IN ANY EVENT PLAINTIFF'S HAVE RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: ROBERT BALIAN, CSR #5220

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES- ATTY. KENNETH M. GREENSTEIN OF GREENSTEIN & MACDONALD, TEL. NO. 773-1240 EXT. 304 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ANDREW M. KLIMENKO OF COLOMBATTO, KLIMENKO, LLP. TEL. NO. 391-6182 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY YICK WONG.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT YICK WONG 'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ SUMMARY ADJUDICATION- MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ISSUES BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S HAVE NOT CARRIED THEIR BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT NO ACTIONABLE CONDUCT OCCURED WITHIN THE APPLICABLE STATUTORY PERIODS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THE PLEADING CANNOT SUPPORT THE CAUSE OF ACTION OR THAT A PRIVILEGE NECESSARILY BARS THE CLAIM. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S HAVE NOT CARRIED THEIR BURDEN AND IN ANY EVENT PLAINTIFF'S HAVE RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Robert Balian, CSR # 5220 =301/PJB/AL

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-26-2007 Continued To Nov-01-07 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301

MOTION TO COMPEL ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION
Off Calendar Sep-28-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiffs Laura Andrews, Megan Wilson and Sondra Mayer's Motion To Compel Defendant Yick O. Wong To Appear At Deposition And To Answer Questions At Deposition, To Request Appointment Of A Discovery Referee is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as Defendant is ordered to appear for additional sessions of his deposition until completed. The motion is denied as it seeks the appointment of a discovery referee. If Plaintiffs wish, they may resume Defendant's deposition in room 612 on afternoons as arranged with the clerk of the Discovery Department. The motion is denied as it seeks to compel an answer to questions about his ownership of other residential properties in the Bay Area. The court will not make a determination on any extensions of the discovery cut-off on this motion. All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice And Motion For Mental Examination Of Pltfs Laura Andrews And Sondra Mayer And For Sanctions; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Sep-26-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:


Appearance:
David Semel, Esq., for Yick Wong, Defendant/Moving Party
Ariel M. Gershon, Esq., for Laura Andrews, Plaintiff/Opposing Party

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Defendant Yick Wong's Motion For Mental Examination Of Plaintiffs Laura Andrews And Sondra Mayer is denied as to Plaintiff Sondra Mayer as the court does not find good cause and as the court finds that Sondra Mayer stipulates that no claim is being made over and above that usually associated with the injuries claimed and as she does not intend to present expert testimony in support of her claim of damages.
The motion is conditionally denied as to Plaintiff Laura Andrews as the court finds that Laura Andrews stipulates that no claim is being made over and above that usually associated with the injuries claimed. The order is conditioned upon her entering a stipulation that she does not intend to present expert testimony in support of her claim of damages.
Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.

Modification: Plaintiff has one week to determine whether she intends to enter the stipulation. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
